Authors: Szewczyk R.; Szałatkiewicz J.; Hasiak M.; Ostaszewska-Liżewska A.; Gazda P.; Råback P.; Nowicki M.
Author full names: Szewczyk, Roman (6701849300); Szałatkiewicz, Jakub (55886106500); Hasiak, Mariusz (6603792713); Ostaszewska-Liżewska, Anna (57210387516); Gazda, Piotr (57193521196); Råback, Peter (55951169300); Nowicki, Michał (55963342700)
Title: Magnetic shielding limitations during degaussing and thermal alternatives for data sanitization in hard disk drives
Source title: Journal of Magnetism and Magnetic Materials
Abstract: Complete demagnetization and domain randomization of magnetic recording media are essential for secure data sanitization of decommissioned hard disk drives. This study investigates the effectiveness of electromagnetic degaussing compared with thermal demagnetization, quantifying a previously unaddressed factor: frequency-dependent electromagnetic shielding by conductive HDD enclosures. A three-dimensional finite element model of a 3.5” HDD predicts magnetic field transmission from an external degausser to the recording surface across 5 Hz to 20 kHz, revealing attenuation of three orders of magnitude at frequencies typical of commercial AC degaussers (10 to 20 kHz). A survey of 15 commercial products shows that operating frequency is rarely disclosed, and current data sanitization requirements specify only minimum applied field strength without addressing frequency-dependent shielding. Magnetic force microscopy of degaussed platters corroborates the simulations, showing residual magnetic contrast despite certified treatment, whereas thermally demagnetized samples heated above the Curie point exhibit complete domain randomization with no detectable track structure. A comparative assessment of electromagnetic degaussing versus thermal destruction is presented, considering field delivery effectiveness, verification confidence, component reusability, and environmental impact. These findings expose a compliance gap in which certified degaussing equipment may render drives inoperable while leaving magnetically recoverable data intact. Copyright © 2024. Authors: Zhang Z.; Hu Y.; Li X.; Wang F.; Li Y.; Kunwar A.
Title: Understanding the mechanothermally superior nanotwinned copper: Fabrication procedure, mechanistic models and technological applications
Source title: Advances in Colloid and Interface Science
Abstract: The rapid evolution of microelectronics requires materials that combine exceptional strength, ductility, and electrical conductivity for joining applications and durable lithium-ion battery anodes. Nanotwinned Cu (nt-Cu) surpasses conventional strengthening approaches, which often compromise ductility and conductivity, by using nanoscale twin boundaries to enhance both mechanical and electrical properties. This review examines the thermomechanical characteristics, fabrication methods, multiscale mechanistic insights, and technological applications of nt-Cu, bridging fundamental science with engineering practice. Using an informatics-driven approach that incorporates advanced text mining and visualization techniques, this study elucidates key trends in the structural and mechanical properties of nt-Cu, advancing its application in cutting-edge joining technologies. © 2025 The Authors
